head.title: Before the choir.
head.intro: Christa leads sacred music across New York and New Jersey, as music director of the Higher Word Orchestra and director of music for several parishes and schools.

sections.alt: Christa Dalmazio conducting the Higher Word Choir
sections.quote: Masterworks, and the stories behind them.
sections.source: Conducting the Higher Word Choir · New York City

paragraphs.text: As music director and chorus conductor of the Higher Word Orchestra, Manhattan's roughly sixty-voice Catholic ensemble, Christa helps shape concerts that pair masterworks with the stories of the composers who wrote them, performed in some of the city's most beautiful churches, including the Basilica of St. Patrick's Old Cathedral, St. Agnes, and St. Anthony of Padua.

With the Catholic artist collective Arthouse2B, she has worked as music director and harpist, including the immersive Ruah: Breath of Life, staged at St. Joseph of Greenwich Village and Harlem's Church of the Annunciation, where she performed the music of Hildegard von Bingen on the harp.

Closer to home, she directs music week to week for parishes and schools across New Jersey, training choirs of every age — grade-school singers through adult cantors — on chant, polyphony, and the wider Catholic tradition. In December 2025 her Academy of Our Lady Sacred Music Choir, forty children in grades two through seven, sang as a prelude to the Christmas Spectacular at Radio City Music Hall.

posts.place: Our Lady of Mt. Carmel School & Lumen Gentium Academy

sections.alt: Christa Dalmazio conducting the Academy of Our Lady Sacred Music Choir at Radio City Music Hall
sections.quote: Forty young voices at Radio City.
sections.source: Academy of Our Lady Sacred Music Choir · Christmas Spectacular prelude · December 2025
